title: Asemonea tenuipes
author: Magar, Kiran Thapa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: 1.  Asemonea tenuipes  (O. Pickard-Cambridge, 1869) (Figures 2-3)    Material examined . Continent: Asia; country: Nepal; province: Bagmati; district: Chitwan; locality: Kasara sector, Chitwan National Park; habitat: forest; elevation: 178 m asl; latitude: 27.5382N; longitude: 84.3385E; sampling protocol: vegetation beating; sex: 1 female.   Global distribution . Andaman Islands, India, Myanmar, Nepal (new record), Singapore, Sri Lanka and Thailand.   Identification.  Identification of the female  A. tenuipes  was based on the unique appearance of pale yellow in colouration of the entire body with greenish blue or brown spots on the dorsum of abdomen (after Tay & Li 2010).   Habitat notes . This specimen was found on the leaf of a Sal tree ( Shorea robusta ).
